mnemonics: My Juicy Pie: MJP stands for Model, Judge, Present, the core workflow. Just like making a pie: you create the model (make the filling), judge its quality (taste test), and present the results (serve the pie).
keyPoints: The module focuses on building predictive models using R.
It covers techniques for evaluating model performance and accuracy.
It concludes with best practices for presenting data insights to stakeholders.
commonMistakes: Skipping model validation and judging performance, leading to unreliable predictions.
Presenting raw model output without translating it into actionable business insights.
Using an overly complex model when a simpler one would be just as effective (overfitting).
